title: Initialization, Indeterminate Values, and Undefined Behavior · Object-Lifetime Lens: Indeterminate Value Risk
summary: An optimized release makes an uninitialized-state bug disappear in debug and reappear with a different compiler. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem indeterminate value risk increment in defined-state initialization audit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Avoid evaluating indeterminate values and understand when unsigned-char inspection is and is not permitted.
Initialize every read-reachable scalar, member, padding-sensitive operation, and error-path state before use.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Indeterminate Value Risk · Theory Indeterminate Value Risk
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 1373. Produce bounded C evidence to read only fields proven initialized by state; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
